components.title: WAN Link (Site-to-Site)
components.description: Encrypted site-to-site wide area network (WAN) link connecting organisation office locations or data centres. Implemented as an MPLS or SD-WAN circuit with IPsec encryption overlay to protect data in transit between sites. Traffic between sites traverses the WAN link through the site firewalls with appropriate access control rules.

props.name: encryption
props.value: IPsec IKEv2 with AES-256-GCM and SHA-384

protocols.name: ipsec-ike
protocols.title: IKEv2 — IPsec key exchange and SA negotiation

protocols.name: ipsec-nat-t
protocols.title: IPsec NAT-T — IPsec encapsulation when NAT is present

protocols.name: ipsec-esp
protocols.title: IPsec ESP — Encrypted data transport (IP protocol 50, not TCP/UDP)

port-ranges.remarks: ESP is IP protocol 50 — permit via firewall policy, not port-based rule

implemented-requirements.description: The WAN link configuration is managed under the organisation's change and configuration management plan. IPsec SA parameters, routing configurations, and firewall rules for the WAN link are version-controlled. Changes to WAN link configuration require approval via the ITSM change process.

WAN link status events (tunnel up/down, IKEv2 renegotiation failures, traffic anomalies) are logged by the site firewalls and forwarded to the SIEM. Persistent tunnel-down events trigger on-call alerts.
